canto_title: The Dark Wood and the Encounter with Virgil

scenes.summary: Dante describes his state of spiritual confusion and how he lost the straight path in a dark forest.

scenes.scene_name: The Sight of the Sunlit Hill
scenes.summary: Dante reaches the foot of a hill and sees its summit illuminated by the rays of the sun.
The sight of the light causes Dante's intense fear to subside momentarily.
Dante compares his traumatized spirit to a swimmer looking back at a dangerous sea.
A spotted leopard appears and blocks Dante's path, forcing him to turn back.
The time of day is established as early morning, bringing a brief moment of hope through the cosmic order.
A hungry and fierce lion appears, causing the very air to tremble with its rage.
A starving she-wolf appears, driving Dante back down into the darkness of the valley.
As Dante falls, he sees a faint figure and cries out to him for mercy.
The stranger identifies himself as a Roman poet who sang of the Trojan wars.
The stranger questions why Dante has returned to such trouble instead of climbing the mountain.
Dante realizes with shame that the figure is the great poet Virgil.
Dante praises Virgil as his master and begs him to protect him from the beasts.
Virgil warns that the she-wolf is a greedy beast that will never be satisfied.
Virgil foretells the coming of a hound who will finally bring an end to the wolf's hunger.
The prophecy links the end of the beast to the salvation of the Italian people.
Virgil accepts the role of guide and describes the sights of Hell and Purgatory.
Virgil explains that he cannot lead Dante to the summit because of his past rebellion against imperial rule.
Dante thanks Virgil and commits to the journey, hoping to reach the gates of Heaven.
Virgil begins to move, and Dante follows closely behind him.
